title: opt_clean: Driver-driver conflict warning on unknown module
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

`./yosys -p "proc; opt -fast" conflict.v -q`

```
module top(input clk);
wire ce = 1'b1;
reg q = 1'b0;
always @(posedge clk)
    if (ce) q <= 1'b0;

(* keep *)
unknown_module u(.i(q));
endmodule
```

Produces the warning: `Warning: Driver-driver conflict for \q between cell u.i and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.`

## ~Expected~ Desired behavior

I've been thinking, and right now I can't see why this warning message would be helpful.
1. If connection was to an output port, then resolving it to be constant `1'b0` doesn't help the situation.
2. If it was an input port, then this is perfectly legitimate and nothing needed to be done anyway.

Is it reasonable to suppress this warning for unknown modules?

## Actual behavior

Produces the warning: `Warning: Driver-driver conflict for \q between cell u.i and constant 1'0 in top: Resolved using constant.`

#####

Actually, this came about when I was trying to create instantiations of temporary `$__name_` modules, and then when `opt` was run on them, I was getting these errors.
